message:
fix(core): downgrade known-benign parity-cert fallback warnings to DEBUG (#12)

In parity-cert mode, SELECT patterns like WITH clauses, JOINs, GROUP BY,
window functions, and MATCH operators correctly fall back to in-memory
evaluation. These are expected behaviors, not errors.

Previously all fallbacks logged at WARN level, creating noise in logs.
Now known-benign fallback reasons log at DEBUG, while unexpected fallbacks
and strict-reject mode still log at WARN/error appropriately.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
